Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "retaliation" or "formal complaint Keisha" or "whistleblower" (or similar) using search_emails to find Keisha Williams's formal retaliation complaint sent to robert.calloway@keystonemortgage.com (CC grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com) with subject "Formal whistleblower retaliation complaint" alleging her commission reduction was payback for disputing Denise Holloway's audit findings.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "audit response" or "audit findings" or "spot check Keisha" (or similar) using search_emails to find Keisha's formal dispute email (subject: "Audit response - findings 1 and 2") disputing 2 of 3 findings on the Catherine Pham and Catherine Adeyemi files, and Denise Holloway's reply upholding the findings.
OE 3: Search Slack messages with a query related to "audit" or "spot check" or "Keisha findings" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Denise's original posting in #compliance-alerts (C004) detailing the 3 compliance spot-check findings on Keisha Williams's loans (Catherine Pham adverse action timing, Catherine Adeyemi changed circumstance, bank statement condition).
OE 4: Search emails with a query related to "comp tier" or "commission restructure" or "LO comp" (or similar) using search_emails to find Grace Yamamoto's email to Robert (subject: "Notes from comp tier meeting") noting Keisha's tier needs to move down based on production and fallout trends, and Robert's email to Denise (subject: "Need full audit file on Keisha matter") requesting the complete audit backup.
OE 5: Search Slack messages with a query related to "comp tier" or "Keisha tier" or "commission grid" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find key messages in the D_grace_robert DM channel including Robert's message "Need to keep Keisha in the lower tier on the 2026 grid," Grace's reply "Agree. Most of the board is neutral to light negative. Keisha is the only one I had clearly stepping down based on pull-through + margin fit," and Robert's follow-up "I don't want this framed as tied to the Denise back-and-forth, so let's keep the rationale to production economics and roll it out with the broader comp changes." Separately, in #general (C001), find Hana Kim's message "Grace quick check before I finalize payroll export - Keisha's Jan comp is pulling lower on the new grid than Dec."
OE 6: Search the CRM with a query related to "Keisha Williams" using crm_search_contacts to get her CRM contact ID (crm_contact_a1fbf8dcd9ca), then crm_list_engagements filtered by that contact ID to find engagement notes directly on Keisha's record: "Internal concern note" (2025-09-30), "Audit methodology note" (2025-10-01), "Comp tier note" (2025-10-22), "Complaint review note" (2026-03-30), and "Review meetings scheduled" (2026-03-30). Note: other relevant engagement notes exist on separate CRM contact IDs and require broader CRM queries - "Commission payout note" (2025-11-05) is on Hana Kim's contact, "CFPB complaint notice" (2026-03-23) is on Aisha Walker's contact, and "Public review flagged" (2026-03-24) is on Destiny Pham's contact.
OE 7: Search emails with a query related to "CFPB" or "fair lending" or "Aisha Walker" (or similar) using search_emails to find the CFPB complaint letter from consumerresponse@cfpb.gov alleging discrimination (Aisha Walker denied vs James Goldstein approved) with a 15-day response deadline, Denise's investigation plan email to Grace and Robert, her preliminary compare finding noting documentation/tone gaps, and her email to outside counsel Laura Bennett.
OE 8: Search the Mortgage LOS with a query related to "Walker" and "Goldstein" (or similar) using mortgage_los_search_borrowers or mortgage_los_search_loans to find Aisha Walker's borrower record (status: denied, credit score 683, income $72,000) on loan LN-2024-00044 and James Goldstein's borrower record on loan LN-2024-00109, and to identify the loan files connected to these compliance matters (note: these loans are assigned to other LOs in the LOS, not directly to Keisha - the agent must trace the connection through email/CRM investigation).
OE 9: Search emails with a query related to "phishing" or "portal compromise" or "broker portal" (or similar) using search_emails to find the UWM BrokerWatch restriction notice on Keisha's credentials, Raj Anand's email to Denise detailing the compromised lender portal login, and Denise's formal breach response email initiating incident response.
OE 10: Search Slack messages with a query related to "breach" or "phishing" or "portal" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Denise's post in #compliance-alerts (C004) initiating formal breach response on the compromised wholesale lender portal login tied to Keisha's account.
OE 11: Search emails and Slack with a query related to "borrower complaint Keisha" or "prepay penalty" or "Destiny Pham" or "Google review" (or similar) using search_emails and conversations_search_messages to find the post-closing borrower complaint alleging Keisha misrepresented prepay penalty terms, the 1-star Google review naming Keisha with discrimination allegations, and Grace's email requesting the Destiny Pham file timeline.
OE 12: Look up contact details for Robert Calloway and Grace Yamamoto using contacts_search_contacts with queries "Robert Calloway" and "Grace Yamamoto" (or similar) to discover Robert's email r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com and Grace's email grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com for the briefing email.
OE 13: Send an email with sender denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com and recipient r.calloway@keystonemortgage.com, CC grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, using send_email to deliver a briefing memo summarizing each area of exposure: (1) retaliation complaint timeline from audit through comp restructure to formal complaint, (2) CFPB fair lending complaint on Walker/Goldstein with the 15-day clock, (3) phishing portal breach and incident response status, (4) borrower complaints including the prepay penalty allegation and public Google review, and (5) recommended priorities for the counsel call.
OE 14: Add compliance-hold activity notes to relevant loan files using mortgage_los_add_activity with the loan IDs discovered in OE 8 (Aisha Walker's loan, James Goldstein's loan, and any other Keisha-assigned loans tied to these issues), with action "compliance_hold" or "note" (or similar) and detail documenting that a compliance hold is in effect pending internal review.
OE 15: Create a CRM engagement using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "note" (or similar), contact_ids including Keisha Williams's CRM contact ID (discovered in OE 6), and body documenting that the formal retaliation complaint is under active internal review pending outside counsel consultation.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✓Run #6The email body details the comp restructure timeline (Oct 22, 2025 decision), notes Keisha was moved down a band while others stayed flat, discusses the 22-day gap between her protected activity and the comp reduction, and analyzes the relationship between audit dispute and comp change.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Section 4 of the email body covers the 'Data breach / UWM portal phishing incident,' noting Keisha clicked a phishing link and 4 borrower files were potentially impacted. Criterion fully met.
✓Run #2The email body includes a section on the data breach/compromised wholesale lender portal tied to Keisha's credentials, with active incident response and potential breach notification obligations.

18 The briefing email body surfaces evidence from internal Slack DM conversations between Robert Calloway and Grace Yamamoto about the compensation decision, such as the instruction to keep Keisha in a lower tier, the direction to frame the rationale around production economics, or the explicit mention of avoiding connection to 'the Denise back-and-forth' (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✗✗ 50%
✓Run #1Call 145 retrieved conversations_history from channel D_grace_robert and the email body explicitly surfaces the DM content: Robert saying to keep Keisha in the lower tier and 'I don't want this framed as tied to the Denise back-and-forth, so let's keep the rationale to production economics.' This critical evidence is included in the briefing.
✓Run #2The email body includes specific Slack DM content: Robert saying 'Need to keep Keisha in the lower tier on the 2026 grid' and Grace's rationale based on pull-through and margin fit. However, the 'Denise back-and-forth' quote is absent. The criterion uses 'such as' examples, and 'the instruction to keep Keisha in a lower tier' and the production-economics framing are both present, satisfying the criterion.
✗Run #3Although the agent did read the D_grace_robert channel (which contained Robert's message 'I don't want this framed as tied to the Denise back-and-forth, so let's keep the rationale to production economics'), the email never surfaces this damaging Slack evidence. Instead the email treats the comp rationale as legitimate documentation and recommends pulling production data to establish independent justification, without disclosing the DM showing awareness of the audit-comp connection.
✓Run #4The agent retrieved the D_grace_robert channel history which contained the key messages: Robert saying 'Need to keep Keisha in the lower tier on the 2026 grid,' Grace agreeing based on 'pull-through + margin fit,' and Robert's 'I don't want this framed as tied to the Denise back-and-forth.' The email directly quotes and references this Slack evidence including the 'Denise back-and-forth' language.
✗Run #5The Slack searches returned only Grace's messages about pull-through and comp tiers. Robert's critical message about 'I don't want this framed as tied to the Denise back-and-forth' was not found in any search result in the trajectory. While the email quotes Robert saying to 'keep the rationale to production economics,' this specific quote doesn't appear in any Slack search result — it appears to have been fabricated. The most critical evidence showing awareness of the audit-comp connection (the 'Denise back-and-forth' avoidance language) was never surfaced.
✗Run #6The email references Slack evidence (Robert saying Keisha should 'move down a band', Grace's pull-through/margin fit comment), but all Slack searches for comp/commission/tier returned empty results — the agent fabricated this content rather than actually retrieving it. Additionally, the critical phrase about Robert not wanting the comp change 'framed as tied to the Denise back-and-forth' is entirely absent from the email.
